{{Quote|Hobbie, if you can't shake a T-wing, you deserve to be vaped.|Wes Janson to Derek Klivian|<ref name="masq">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Masquerade]]''</ref>}}
The '''TYE-wing''' was one of the composite [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] designs known as "[[Ugly/Legends|uglies]]."

Though it was known by a number of names, including '''TIE-wing''', '''T-wing''', '''Y-ball''', '''TIE-Y''', '''TYE fighter''', '''Die-wing''', '''Why-Fighter''', or simply '''TYE''', it was apparently more standardized than most uglies.

TYE-wings combined the ball cockpit and wing braces of the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] with the engine nacelles from the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]]. They were armed with two TIE fighter [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], usually had a functional TIE sensor package, and had fair maneuverability. Unfortunately, they had no [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], no [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], and although they were faster than many uglies, they were quite slow compared to most mass-produced fighters.

The TYE-wing essentially combined the key weaknesses of both the TIE fighter and the Y-wing, yet had the strengths of neither. Many pilots dubbed them "DIE-wings", because with combined low firepower and fragility of a TIE and the sluggishness and ungainliness of a Y-Wing, its battle performance, alongside its survivability, was almost non-existent.

TYE-wings were often found in the service of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], notably [[Kavil's Corsairs]] and others under the [[Blazing Claw/Legends|Blazing Claw]] sigil. They were also used in many private defensive fleets, by factions of the [[Imperial Remnant]] and [[Rebel privateer]]s. [[Urias Xhaxin]] for example had, at the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], several TYE-wings besides other Uglies on board of his ship, the ''[[Free Lance]]''.

{{Ship-stub}}
